steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $(0,b)$ is the $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Substitute the given information
steps.content: The slope is $39$, so $m=39$. The graph passes through $(0,0)$, so the $y$-intercept is $b=0$. Therefore $$f(x)=39x+0=39x.$$

steps.title: Check the answer choices
steps.content: $f(x)=-39x$ has slope $-39$, $f(x)=\frac{1}{39}x$ has slope $\frac{1}{39}$, and $f(x)=x-39$ has slope $1$ with intercept $-39$. The function $f(x)=39x$ has slope $39$ and passes through the origin.
